title: Sprint-1 — Mirror+Verify+Flip+Distill (vertical slice)
notes: GATE PASSED (bd-3n1 GO, running spike, 2026-06-01). REFINED SEAM: Better Auth owns person/wallet-group-STORAGE/world-membership/sessions/JWT (account×N per user proven). SOVEREIGN owns: the ACCOUNT/inventory/TBA layer + the CROSS-WALLET LINKING CEREMONY (Better Auth doesn't merge distinct wallets from a session — verify 2nd SIWE sig → attach account to authed user). Adopt better-auth@1.6.13 (Drizzle/Postgres), jwt.definePayload for per-world claims, internal getSchema() for schema-gen (CLI drift). NEXT: re-scope the SDD to this seam before building — the build shrinks vs the May-1 hand-built canonical-user plan.

comments.author: soju
comments.text:
# Auth Vertical — Decision Reconciled (corrects the 2026-06-01 confabulated brief)

**date:** 2026-06-01 · **status:** operator-ratified · **supersedes the "drop & adopt Better Auth as issuer/SoR" framing in** `grimoires/loa/specs/enhance-auth-vertical-betterauth-seam.md` + `grimoires/loa/2026-06-01-auth-vertical-rescope.md`

## What happened (the confabulation + its mechanism)

The `bd-2wo` child tasks (`.1`–`.32`) were authored 2026-05-01 as a *plan*. The work was then **built off-bead** on branches `feat/t1.x` → `w2.5-sprint-3-auth-sdk-source-distributed` (through `/fagan` iter-3, `dd60990`) **without bead lifecycle** — so the children stayed `open`. A 2026-06-01 `/kickoff` agent read the open beads as an **unbuilt plan** and produced a brief directing us to *drop the "May-1 hand-built" canonical-user / jwks / credential-bridges and adopt Better Auth as the issuer/SoR.* That inverted reality: the artifacts are **shipped, reviewed code**, not an empty plan. The brief is superseded by this record.

Root cause: the SoT (beads) lied. Reconciled below.

## The corrected decision

- **Keep the hand-built ES256 sovereign stack as the issuer / SoR** (LocalEs256Signer, JWKS, Postgres spine, credential bridges, source-distributed verify SDK). This is what **PRD v3.0** already specifies (local ES256 signer behind the `JWTSigner` port; identity-api as SoR).
- **Better Auth = a per-world CREDENTIAL ADAPTER** behind the existing `CredentialBridge` port — translating Better Auth proof (passkey / social / email) → canonical `CredentialProof`. This is the operator's actual goal (**multi-credential login + standardized per-world login UX**) and is **PRD v3.0-sanctioned** ("Better Auth deferred to a per-world adapter", `prd.md:315,323`).
- **NOT** an issuer/SoR replacement. Better Auth is a composed library at the credential **ingress** edge — never an absorbed runtime, never touching the ES256 svc-JWT **egress** contract.
- The **account/character/TBA/inventory model stays KAIRONIC** (held open) — this cycle firms the substrate under it, does not decide it.

## Evidence (cluster probe, confidence 0.93 · 2026-06-01)

- **ZERO external consumers** of the ES256 svc-JWT verify contract cluster-wide. The "1 vendored consumer" (`fa-c2-world-managers`) is our **own git worktree** (byte-identical, same object store). The source-distributed `@0xhoneyjar/auth` SDK is unmerged (sprint-3 branch only). → **contract-change window is fully OPEN** — cheapest moment to make any breaking ES256-contract change, until a real world first vendors the source.
- **Current prod auth is Dynamic SDK end-to-end** (NOT siwe-turso — both the brief and an early read were wrong): midi (`mibera-dimensions`, Vercel) issues+verifies Dynamic JWTs and is the de-facto SoR/writer; service-to-service is static SHA-256 API keys; no svc-JWT in any live path.
- **identity-api is live but build-lagged**: `identity.0xhoneyjar.xyz` serves only the HS256 login/resolve spine; the merged ES256 `service-jwt` + `/.well-known/jwks.json` routes **404** (Railway deploy predates the W2.5 sprint-2 merges). Its one consumer (`freeside-dashboard`) is itself undeployed. → nothing real depends on the new stack yet.
- **Shadow-mode confirmed by design**: the `AUTH_BACKEND` mirror→verify→flip (`bd-2wo.22`–`.29`) keeps Dynamic live; the new substrate flips only on operator GO. No cutover pressure (timeline: soon, no hard date).

## Blast-radius map

| Move | Blast radius |
|---|---|
| ADD Better Auth credential adapter behind `CredentialBridge` | **near-zero** — doesn't touch signer/JWKS/contract or prod |
| Change the ES256 svc-JWT contract | **empty now**; coordinated cross-repo re-vendor once a world vendors the SDK |
| Change identity-api runtime internals | **local** to the producer repo |

## Bead reconciliation (`bd-2wo`)

- **CLOSED — shipped** (evidence in tree): `.1` `protocol/user.ts`(+schema) · `.3` `protocol/jwt-claims.ts` · `.6` `adapters/migrations/0001_init_spine` · `.10` `adapters/jwks-validator.ts` · `.11` `adapters/credential-bridge-siwe.ts` · `.5` `protocol/VERSIONING.md` (source-distributed model superseded npm-publish; pkg `private:true`) · `.7` `ports/*.port.ts` (evolved naming: spine/jwt-signer/jwt-verifier vs old IUserRepo/ICredentialRepo) · `.8` `engine/resolve-spine.ts` + `link-verified-wallet.ts` (mint/link; revoke via credential `revoked_at`) · `.9` `engine/resolve-spine.ts` · `.18` `adapters/postgres-{spine,split}-adapter.ts` + `denylist-postgres.ts`.
- **PARTIAL — kept open**: `.2` (credential schema: `credential-dynamic` shipped; generic append-only `CredentialProof` to finalize with the adapter) · `.15` (`credential-bridge-dynamic.ts` shipped; the ESLint guard blocking `@dynamic-labs/*` not yet in place).
- **FUTURE — kept open**: `.4` world-manifest-auth schema (absent) · `.12` passkey + `.13` discord-bot bridges (now subsumed by the Better Auth adapter scope) · `.16` `pg-mibera-profiles` read · `.17` `dynamic-csv-translator` (the ~98k import) · `.19`/`.20`/`.21` mcp-tools (agent surface, not built) · `.22`–`.29` Mirror/Verify/Flip cutover · `.30`–`.33` distill.
- **ACTIVE BUILD**: `.14` `credential-bridge-better-auth` — unblocked (Sprint-0.5 `bd-3n1` PASSED GO), re-scoped to the credential-adapter decision above.
- **Cutover correction** (`.22`–`.29`): the source backend is **Dynamic** (not siwe-turso); Better Auth is **NOT** stood up as an issuer in Mirror — it is a credential adapter only.

## Sequencing

1. **Hygiene** (this record): reconcile beads · close `bd-3n1` (gate GO) · fix the stale "JWKS issuance lives at loa-freeside/apps/gateway" doctrine in `README.md` + `packages/protocol/src/index.ts` (false — the signer is the in-repo `LocalEs256Signer` per PRD v3.0; that gateway is a Discord/NATS gateway with no `/jwks` or `/issue`).
2. **Build** `bd-2wo.14` — Better Auth credential adapter behind `CredentialBridge` — via `code-implement-and-review`.
3. **Window opportunity** (independent): harden the ES256 svc-JWT contract while the change-window is free.
4. **Deferred / operator's call**: redeploy identity-api so the running build stops lagging `origin/main`; the user-JWT `HS256→ES256` swap (signer already exists).

comments.author: soju
comments.text: CONVERGED + reviewed APPROVED (2026-06-01, code-implement-and-review). Scaffold landed: session-gated /v1/link/discord/{initiate,callback} (src/api/routes/discord-link.ts) + Discord OAuth boundary behind an injectable port (src/discord-oauth.ts: signed single-use TTL+sub-bound state) + thin reuse helper (packages/engine/src/link-verified-credential.ts → resolveByAccount + linkAccountWithAudit + LinkCrossUserCollisionError). 20/20 tests. Adversarial review (construct-scar): iter-1 CHANGES_REQUIRED (1 CRITICAL single-use + 2 MAJOR 500-paths + 2 MINOR) → all fixed → iter-2 APPROVED. Scope held: NO CredentialScheme/bridge added; ES256 signer/JWKS//v1/auth/verify untouched; account/character model untouched (kaironic). NOT live yet — DEFERRED to follow-ons: bd-2wo.34 (#6 bearer-vs-cookie auth model — won't complete a real browser redirect as-is), bd-2wo.35 (live exchangeCode + Discord creds), bd-2wo.36 (multi-instance single-use store), bd-2wo.37 (pre-existing BB-F-005 audit-clobber). Spec: grimoires/loa/specs/discord-social-credential-link-adapter.md.

Discord-link OAuth-dance auth model: bearer-vs-cookie for the browser redirect
description: Follow-on from bd-2wo.14. The /v1/link/discord/callback route is .auth() bearer-gated, but a real Discord OAuth redirect is a browser navigation carrying COOKIES, not an Authorization: Bearer header (default extract reads only the header — src/hyper/auth-jwt/index.ts:95). So the dance won't complete a real browser round-trip as built (tests pass only because they fetch with an explicit bearer). DECIDE the auth model: (a) cookie-session — Discord redirects directly to the API, auth via withSession cookie (also gives a natural pendingNonce single-use store; needs shared session store for multi-instance); or (b) SPA-recall — DISCORD_LINK_CALLBACK_URL points at the front-end, the SPA reads code+state and POSTs to the API WITH the bearer (keeps the JWT model consistent). Confirmed independently by construct-scar. Creds-gated/deferred to front-end integration.

title: Discord-link: wire live exchangeCode (Better Auth / raw Discord OAuth client) + Discord app creds
description: Follow-on from bd-2wo.14. defaultDiscordOAuthClient.exchangeCode currently throws DiscordOAuthNotProvisioned (→503) — the live code-exchange is deferred. Wire a real client behind the DiscordOAuthClient port (src/discord-oauth.ts): either Better Auth's Discord social provider (library-mode, NOT mounted as the app runtime) or a raw Discord OAuth token-exchange. Requires a Discord application: DISCORD_CLIENT_ID / DISCORD_CLIENT_SECRET / DISCORD_LINK_CALLBACK_URL. Map live exchange failures to 4xx/502 (route already does). Then the route flow goes live.

title: Discord-link single-use state: shared-store backing for multi-instance prod
description: Follow-on from bd-2wo.14. tryConsumeStateNonce (src/discord-oauth.ts) records consumed nonces in an in-process Map — single-instance only (same limitation as the default memorySessions store). For multi-instance prod, back single-use with a shared store (spine table / Redis) so a replay can't land on a second instance. Documented caveat at discord-oauth.ts; live is creds-gated until then.

title: linkAccountWithAudit: wrap aborted-txn audit-write (BB-F-005 pattern) so it doesn't clobber the typed error
description: Pre-existing (NOT introduced by bd-2wo.14; affects link-verified-wallet path too). In real PG, when spine.linkAccount raises the racing unique-violation inside the txn, linkAccountWithAudit's catch writes conflict_rejected on the ALREADY-ABORTED connection → throws 25P02 and clobbers the original SpineConflictError (resolve-spine.ts:196-207). Harmless today (link-verified-credential's outer re-resolve decides recovery; status still correct) but it degrades the 5xx diagnostic on the genuine-failure branch. Fix: wrap the audit-write in try/catch and re-throw the ORIGINAL error — mirror the BB-F-005 pattern already in link-verified-wallet.ts:242-264. Surfaced by construct-scar iter-2 re-review.

title: v1 POST /v1/identity/resolve merge facade (ratified #32)
description:
Ratified 2026-06-01 (issue #32, comment posted). Server-side merge facade: dashboard POSTs wallets[] -> identity-api joins spine (resolveByWallet->getIdentity: user_id, world nym, discord id, linked) + enriches via score-api, returns ONE pre-merged identity per wallet so the dashboard re-derives nothing.

TWO bounded tasks:
1. NEW ScorePort.resolveIdentity + HttpScoreAdapter call to score-api POST /v1/identity/resolve (the onchain name chain; the existing wired client is bound to GET /v1/wallets/:address = scores-only). Same building/auth, no new infra.
2. NEW POST /v1/identity/resolve route (Pattern B): join spine + score enrich, apply priority server-side ONCE.

RATIFIED CONTRACT (see #32 comment + the to-be-written spec):
- priority: world_nym > discord(id-only) > score-api display_name > address. Do NOT re-derive beraname>ENS>twitter (consume score's display_name as one tier; keep raw fields as passthrough tooltips).
- discord: { id, linked } only (NO handle — spine has no username column).
- reachable: true|false|unknown tri-state (unknown until #11 P1).
- per-wallet degraded flag (score enrichment unavailable).
- grouping SoR = identity-api wallet_links/primary_wallet; score-api enriches per-wallet.
- wallet-keyed POST, wallets <=100.

GATES: feature work -> Loa gate (NOT /bug). Ship behind contract-first bridge (IDENTITY_RESOLVE_URL). Build can proceed in PARALLEL with #11; dashboard CUTOVER holds for #11 P1 + world-identity backfill coverage (prod today: 5 users / 3 nyms — backfill ran but spine coverage tiny). Held-open: per-world nym vs unified freeside-nym (future identity-model decision).

title: Component 1: ScorePort.resolveIdentity port method + HttpScoreAdapter POST binding + sealed score schema
description:
SDD Phase A (T-A1, T-A2). Component 1 of bd-2wo.38.

T-A1: Author ScoreResolveIdentityReqSchema ({ wallets } only, 0x+40hex, min(1).max(100), NO world_slug — score is wallet-only) + ScoreResolveIdentityRespSchema (KEYED MAP { identities: Record<lowercased-wallet, ResolvedIdentity> }, .loose() forward-compat) in packages/protocol/src/api/federation/score.ts (sibling to ScoreGetWalletRespSchema at federation/score.ts:109-164, which confirms the .loose() pattern). ResolvedIdentity per SDD §1.6: wallet, display_name (non-null), ens_name|null, beraname|null, basename|null, twitter_handle|null, pfp_url|null, twitter_source|null. Re-export from federation/index.ts + api/index.ts.
Add resolveIdentity(input, opts?): Promise<FederationResult<ScoreResolveIdentityResp>> to ScorePort (packages/ports/src/score.port.ts — today has ONE method getScore at :84) with never-throws docstring contract.
Implement HttpScoreAdapter.resolveIdentity via federationHttpCall({ method:'POST', body:{ wallets }, responseSchema, portOpts }) (POST+body confirmed at federation-http.ts:106,116-141). Mirror getScore X-API-Key header + building:'score-api' + context:{ walletCount, hasApiKey }; never log the key.

T-A2: Confirm dashboard-caller auth posture (OQ-3). Default to sibling-read open posture (/v1/profile, /v1/resolve/* use route without .auth()). If protected required: add .auth() to the route ONLY — DO NOT touch the verify implementation (HARD constraint).

TEST-FIRST: §7.1 adapter classification matrix (AC-12) via injected fetchImpl / MockScorePort: 200/401/404/429(breaker-exempt)/5xx/parse/timeout -> correct FederationResult; never throws.

HARD constraint: ES256 signer / JWKS / svc-JWT verify / CredentialBridge stay byte-unchanged.
Source: sdd.md §1.4 (Component 1), §1.6, §5.5, §7.2 case 12.
close_reason: Component 1 shipped: ScorePort.resolveIdentity + HttpScoreAdapter POST binding + sealed keyed-map schema; 10 adapter tests green, typecheck clean

title: Component 2: protocol Req/Resp schemas + pure mergeIdentity priority resolver + POST /v1/identity/resolve route
description:
SDD Phase B (T-B1, T-B2). Component 2 of bd-2wo.38.

T-B1 (schemas + pure resolver, TEST-FIRST): Author IdentityResolveReqSchema (wallets: array(WalletAddressParamSchema).min(1).max(100) reuse resolve.ts:26-28; world_slug: WorldSlugParamSchema.optional() reuse resolve.ts:42-44) + IdentityResolveRespSchema ({ results: array(IdentityResolveEntrySchema) }, per-wallet pinned contract §5.3; reachable as string-enum 'true'|'false'|'unknown' per OQ-5 pin) in packages/protocol/src/api/identity-resolve.ts (NEW); re-export from api/index.ts.
Write PURE mergeIdentity(spine, scoreEnrich, world_slug?) resolver FIRST: 4-tier rule applied ONCE (world_nym > discord(id-only) > score(real-name-only) > address); score tier fires ONLY if beraname/ens_name/twitter_handle non-null (SDD §1.5 ▼); display_source derivation; discord { id, linked } (NO username — col doesn't exist, 0001:67-74); reachable 'unknown' default; raw passthrough beraname/ens/twitter.
TEST-FIRST §7.2 cases 1-3,7-10 (AC-1,2,3,7,10) as pure-fn unit tests.

T-B2 (route, TEST-FIRST): Author src/api/routes/identity-resolve.ts (Pattern B route.post('/v1/identity/resolve').body(ReqSchema).meta().handle() — .body() validation DOES run for POST): (1) validate + lowercase-normalize + dedupe; (2) per wallet resolveByWallet(getSpine())->getIdentity (engine readers used at resolve.ts:100,201); (3) ONE batched getScore().resolveIdentity({ wallets }); lookup resp.identities[wallet.toLowerCase()]; (4) mergeIdentity per wallet; is_primary_wallet from spine, reachable tri-state, per-wallet degraded (computed once per batch); (5) order-stable results[], echo normalized wallet. Wire AbortController with IDENTITY_RESOLVE_SCORE_TIMEOUT_MS (new optional env). Error posture §6.2: validation->400 envelope (profile.ts:81-86); score degrade->200+degraded; spine MISS(null)->address fallback; spine THROW(real DB I/O)->5xx global handler. NO circuit breaker in v1. Register in .use([...]) array at src/api/index.ts.
TEST-FIRST §7.2 cases 4-6,8-9,11 (AC-4,5,6,8,9,11) as integration tests against booted app (ephemeral port seam; __setScoreForTest at score.ts:49).

Depends on bd-2wo.38.1 (sealed score schema). HARD constraint: auth/signer/spine untouched.
Source: sdd.md §1.4 (Component 2), §1.5, §5.2, §5.3, §5.4, §6.2, §7.2.
close_reason: Component 2 shipped: protocol Req/Resp schemas + pure mergeIdentity + POST /v1/identity/resolve route; 16 merge unit + 10 route integration tests green

comments.author: zksoju
comments.text: Discovered issue: bd-9qj - openapi-zod converter (src/hyper/openapi-zod/index.ts:66) crashes on z.array() body schemas. Zod-4 ZodArray _def.type is the 'array' discriminator STRING, but the converter does (v.type ?? v.element) and recurses into the string -> TypeError at defName (index.ts:38). Triggered by the first .body() schema containing an array (POST /v1/identity/resolve). Workaround: route validates body in-handler instead of .body(). Fix: ZodArray case must read _def.element (Zod4) not _def.type.

title: Contract-bridge handoff: sealed response schema importable for dashboard mock-fallback (IDENTITY_RESOLVE_URL)
description:
SDD Phase C (T-C1). Contract bridge of bd-2wo.38.

Confirm IdentityResolveRespSchema is importable so the dashboard can build its IDENTITY_RESOLVE_URL mock-fallback against the sealed shape (SDD §4.1). Document the contract in the source-distributed SDK surface (NO npm dependency — vendored source per PRD lock-in L333, shadcn-style add/registry).

Cutover is GATED on #11 P1 + world-identity backfill coverage (prod today: 5 users / 3 nyms) — NOT this sprint. Shipping the route does NOT trigger cutover.

Depends on bd-2wo.38.2 (sealed response schema).
Source: sdd.md §4, §8 Phase C.

title: E2E goal validation: G-5 read-time-compose / no-embed contract end-to-end
description:
Task 1.E2E (P0). Validate PRD goal G-5 (profile serving — read-time compose, no embed) end-to-end for the merge facade.

Validation:
1. Booted-app integration test: POST a batch with (a) wallet->spine user with world_nym, (b) discord linked + no nym, (c) real score onchain name only, (d) unresolved wallet — assert each display_source tier + merged display_name. One pre-merged identity per wallet, priority applied ONCE.
2. No-embed (FR-P3): git grep for new *.up.sql / migrations; assert route stores nothing. Spine schema unchanged.
3. Degradation (FR-P2 / NFR-2): force score-api timeout -> all entries degraded=true, 200 OK, spine tiers still resolve. Outage degrades enrichment only — never the batch, never auth/resolve.
4. Boundary (score-vs-identity): is_primary_wallet from spine; beraname/ens/twitter raw passthrough not re-ranked.
5. HARD constraint AC-13: git diff shows ZERO changes to LocalEs256Signer / JWKS / svc-JWT verify / /v1/auth/verify / CredentialBridge.

Depends on bd-2wo.38.2 (route).
Source: sdd.md §7, prd.md §4.5 G-5.

title: twitter as first-class identity-api linked_account (OAuth link) + facade source-flip
description:
v2 arc (per #11 comment 2026-06-01, not blocking #32 v1). score-api's twitter_handle is SCRAPED FROM DYNAMIC — the provider #11 replaces. Make twitter a first-class identity-api linked_accounts provider:
- add 'twitter' provider to the linked-account model + an OAuth link flow mirroring /v1/link/discord/* (same Better Auth credential-adapter pattern as bd-2wo.14; reuse the signed-state/single-use/linkVerifiedCredential machinery the Discord adapter established).
- when it lands, the #32 resolve facade (bd-2wo.38) prefers identity-LINKED twitter over score-scraped twitter (verified > inferred); display_source gains a 'twitter' tier above 'score'. SDD already notes this source-flip is designed non-breaking.
- part of the v2 endgame: identity-api owns ALL identity axes (nym, discord, twitter-linked, onchain); score-api returns to pure scoring; the facade hides the migration from consumers.
Reinforces the pinned priority (freeside-owned identity primary; beraname now just-an-ENS, onchain secondary). Sibling of bd-2wo.13 (discord-bot) / .12 (passkey) under the credential-adapter umbrella ([[better-auth-is-a-credential-adapter-not-issuer]]).

title: Task 0.3: Better Auth POC scaffold (3-day timebox; DEC-OPEN-1, RSK-7)
close_reason: RUNNING SPIKE PASS: better-auth@1.6.13 + siwe/jwt/organization on Drizzle/bun:sqlite + viem booted clean. Proved live: SIWE login (verify 200), WALLET-GROUP HOLDS (3 account rows/1 user, queried), per-world JWT {world,tenant,wallet_group_user_id} EdDSA-verified via jwt.definePayload. KEY FINDING: SIWE auto-groups same-address-across-chainIds (1+80094→1 user) but does NOT merge distinct wallets from a session — the cross-wallet LINKING CEREMONY is sovereign code (verify 2nd sig → attach account+walletAddress to authed user). Gotchas: asResponse:true server-side; @better-auth/cli@1.4.21 drift → bypass via internal getSchema(); bun:sqlite over better-sqlite3; Berachain EOA needs no RPC. Spike: /tmp/wt-betterauth-spike/poc/better-auth-spike/ (branch spike/better-auth-poc, untracked).

title: openapi-zod converter (src/hyper/openapi-zod/index.ts:66) crashes on z.array() body schemas. Zod-4 ZodArray _def.type is the 'array' discriminator STRING, but the converter does (v.type ?? v.element) and recurses into the string -> TypeError at defName (index.ts:38). Triggered by the first .body() schema containing an array (POST /v1/identity/resolve). Workaround: route validates body in-handler instead of .body(). Fix: ZodArray case must read _def.element (Zod4) not _def.type.
